title: bearer token exposed in URL when passed as ?token= query param
description: EventSource and WebSocket clients that cannot set Authorization headers pass the bearer token as ?token= in the URL. HTTP access logs (nginx, reverse proxies, load balancers, CDN edge logs) record full URLs including query parameters, so the token appears in plaintext in any log pipeline that sees the request. The token is equivalent to full API access. Fix options: document that the query-param path requires log scrubbing, or rotate token on server restart and enforce short TTL, or add a token exchange endpoint that issues short-lived WebSocket-specific tokens.
